author: @TunnelPlaya
text: hey, i know you uploaded this a few years ago now but, i dont 100% understand your modifications. what it looks like is you made the coil run the full length of the soft iron but what is that box? a magnet? how is it fixed to the glass? what kind of power is required to drive this? i expect i wouldnt get good results if i simply plugged an ipod in directly to the coil

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it's bascially a soundbug mate being driven by an audio amp

author: @hi-kn9gd
text: hoe did you make the sound bug wider, out of what materials? Does this upgrade the quality and volume of your original design?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: hi, i just used a linear array of magnets and longer materials - and yes it up grades quality and volume

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: I really am sorry but I am not likely to do that. Too little time and too much to do. The first step is to become a partner. For that you need to apply to youtube. If they accept you, you can monetize. I don't really know how to do that as YouTube invited me. But I think if you google it there is a lot of online help. And thanks for your kind comments. Sorry I couldn't be more help

author: @thecuddaking
text: By the way thanks in advance if you can. Great videos too. Every single video you post is awesome and valuable info. Thanks again for your work and dedication to teach and share!

author: @thecuddaking
text: A little off topic but I apologize, but could you do a video on how you go about monetizing your youtube? Or email me some info if you don't mind. It's my YouTube name @ gmail.com

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: You wouldn't want to go hitting it with a hammer but it's pretty safe - try toughened glass or plexiglass if you are worried about it - either will work

author: @Nothatsnotnice
text: Cool video Robert! Im wondering however, how safe are these? The last thing I want is for the glass to shatter :)

author: @invisibletenants
text: Greetings, will this work with plexiglass or plastic? I was thinking of making a set of plastic speakers in different colors, which basically would each consist of a simple, long sheet of plastic, that is bent at the bottom so it stands upright on it's own. Do you think it would work with this kind of material?

author: @ThinkingandTinkering
text: Yeah afarid so - i really need a better one.
